reforms.description: The Finnish government has significantly increased R&D support, utilizing part of the privatisation proceeds to achieve a target of R&D spending of 2.9% of GDP by 1999, which was successfully reached. This initiative has contributed to a rise in total research and development spending, positioning Finland among the highest in the OECD.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The target of R&D spending was achieved in 1998, indicating successful implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a structural change in R&D funding policy, significantly impacting the overall research landscape.
reforms.source_quote: In September 1996, the government decided to use part of the privatisation proceeds to foster R&D and it set a target of R&D spending of 2.9 per cent of GDP by 1999 – one of the highest ratios in the OECD area.

reforms.description: The Finnish government aims to maintain overall spending on R&D at least at the current level, focusing on less developed areas as part of regional policy while promoting marketing and commercialization of products.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Maintaining R&D spending is crucial for sustaining innovation and supporting regional development.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has expressed intentions to maintain R&D spending levels.
reforms.importance_rationale: While not a major reform, it represents a significant commitment to R&D funding.
reforms.source_quote: Efforts will be made to maintain the overall spending on R&D at least at the current level, with greater emphasis on the less developed areas as an integral part of regional policy.

reforms.description: The Finnish government is supporting R&D for energy conservation through various instruments, including subsidies for energy-saving investments and technological development related to renewable energy sources.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in energy conservation R&D is expected to foster innovation in sustainable technologies, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is actively supporting R&D for energy conservation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant but narrower focus on energy-related R&D.
reforms.source_quote: The state is supporting R&D for energy conservation.
